level. That might have made it more confusing, but you've got to just think about with CBO. So it's really not something I'm very concerned with because by that point, I mean, unfortunately, it's hard to get a campaign to last longer than that anyways. But if you do, then a solution to that, I mean, it's, yeah, I mean, I don't know. I really don't know the answers to that because I don't think we faced it a lot. I think with boom, usually about that time, things start to fatigue anyways, or we just quit adding new ads in because there's definitely on a rare occasion that we would ever go in and turn off or delete an ad, especially with CBO. It's just not something that we've experienced a ton because, you know, it's rare for a campaign to last, you know, almost a full year, especially just at the volume that we're trying to scale right now. And boom is a pretty big business. So I don't think anyone in here has a business at such a size that they should be running into that issue. But you just said something that I want to clarify because a bunch of people asked this question too. Did you say it is okay to switch off ads in CBO or not okay to do that? No, I rarely ever switch off any ads, period. So and this is back to the breakdown effect. If you guys miss that, go back to module three. Definitely go to the bit.ly link I shared study the breakdown effect.

## [1:15:12 - 1:18:13]

It explains why it's usually a very bad idea to go in and start turning ads off because even if you go and you're like, oh, this ad is the worst. What you don't realize is that halo effect and that all of those ads are working together to produce that conversion or whatever you're asking for. So if you go in and you have four ads live and you turn the three off that are doing the worst, I'm doing my air quotes, you're actually just crushing your campaign in your mind, you might be saving money or it's like, oh, this is optimization. And it's like, no, it's definitely not. You're just kind of crushing your campaign because now Facebook has three less ads to work with. So that's really my thought process. There is a very, very rare occasion that we turn off ads. Like maybe if for some reason the messaging is just completely not working and I just don't feel good about having that ad live as a reflection of a brand that I'm working with. That might be a reason that we turn them off, but very rarely otherwise are we turning ads off. And yeah, especially nowadays with the advancement in machine learning and how smart these platforms are, I feel really passionately about that. And something else that you'll see is that, and I get this all the time, Molly, I turned ads off and then I went back a few weeks later. And I actually found that the ads that I turned off had the highest return on ad spend because I needed to wait a little bit. There was a bit of a delayed attribution and you'll find that too. So yeah, just some thoughts on that, John. Cool. Okay. Well, I know we went kind of deep there. Do you want to maybe do this last question and then we'll save everything else for overflow tomorrow? And then did we get anything else here in the questions box that you feel like I need to address right now? I think you had all the... Yeah, okay. Yeah, okay. Yeah, we've addressed pretty much all of this. Well, so, yeah, no, I think, I think Leia was just saying that if you have an ad with good ROI and an ad with bad ROI, you wouldn't turn the ad with bad ROI off. No, because you've got to study the breakdown effect. You should not be making decisions like that at the ad level. If you're using CBO, you need to make decisions at the campaign level. If you're using budget at the ad set level, you need to make decisions at the ad set level because that is where you are setting the optimization. If you're making those decisions at the ad level, it doesn't make sense in terms of how the platform works. So please, please study the breakdown effect, guys, and don't make decisions at the ad level. They are all there to work together. Yep, I think that makes a lot of sense. And yeah, I recommend everyone that is wanting to go deep on this, go back and re-watch.
